message.thinking:
We need to produce JSON array with 3 objects, one per question. Each object has keys: "question" (string: "Chapters", "Description", or "Structure"), "feedback" (text), "complies" (boolean). Evaluate the given text.

Text: "Darbs ietver PE failu formāta izpēti, lai iegūtu zināšanas par tā daļām, kuras var izmantot informācijas slēpšanai, zināmo metožu un rīku analīzi, kā arī to darbības principu izpratni. Darbā tiks analizēti tadas metodikas, ka Slack Space (brīvas vietas izmantošana), virknu nomaiņa (izmantojot tādus rīkus, kā Hydan un steg86), kā arī dll injekcijas. Praktiskajā daļā tiks mēģināts slēpt informāciju PE failos, izmantojot izpētītās metodes un rīkus, analizējot to efektivitāti un iespējamo ietekmi uz faila funkcionalitāti."

We need to answer:

1. Chapters: Does the text list the main sections of the thesis? The text does not list main sections; it mentions content but not sections. So fails. Feedback: It doesn't list chapters; only mentions topics. So "complies": false.

2. Description: Does the text briefly describe contents of each section? Since no sections listed, cannot describe each. So fails. Feedback: No description of sections. "complies": false.

3. Structure: Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text? It mentions theoretical part (analysis of methods) and practical part (implementation). That gives some structure: theoretical analysis, practical application. But not explicit. The question: "Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text?" I'd say partially: yes, we can infer two parts: theoretical and practical. But the text doesn't clearly separate sections. So maybe "complies": false? But maybe "true" because it mentions theoretical and practical parts. The question: "Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text?" The text says: "Darbs ietver PE failu formāta izpēti, lai iegūtu zināšanas par tā daļām... Analizēti tadas metodikas... Praktiskajā daļā tiks mēģināts slēpt informāciju..." So yes, we can see a structure: theoretical analysis of methods, then practical implementation. So I'd say it is understandable. So "complies": true.

But we need to be consistent: The text doesn't explicitly list chapters, but the structure is implied. So we can say "Structure: yes, general structure is clear." So "complies": true.

Now produce JSON array with 3 objects.

Let's craft feedback:

1. Chapters: "Text does not list any chapter titles or headings; it only mentions topics. Therefore it does not satisfy the requirement to list main sections." "complies": false.

2. Description: "Because no chapters are listed, there is no description of each section. The text merely outlines topics but does not describe contents per section." "complies": false.

3. Structure: "The text indicates a theoretical part (analysis of methods) and a practical part (implementation). This gives a general sense of structure, though explicit chapter names are missing." "complies": true.

Return array. Ensure JSON syntax.
